title: json_skip_string silently accepts invalid escapes that json_parse_string rejects
description: Trigger: JSON with an invalid single-char escape (e.g. \z, \a, \0) in a non-extracted field, e.g. {"other":"\z","password":"hunter2"}. Bad behavior: extract_field("password") succeeds because json_skip_string falls through to Some(_) => {} for all single-char escapes; extract_field("other") fails because json_parse_string returns Err for unknown escapes. The same JSON document produces different results depending on which field you extract — this is inconsistent validation. A strictly invalid JSON document may be silently accepted as long as the invalid part is not in the target field.
